alert.body: Verify whether SDS is available for the client's country of residence before promising timelines. Use Non-SDS when SDS is paused or ineligible.

about.label: Description
about.value: Study permit allowing international students to study at a Designated Learning Institution (DLI) in Canada. Application is typically online via IRCC portal; biometrics and medical may apply.

about.label: Eligible applicants
about.value: Valid LOA from DLI · Proof of funds · Clean immigration history · Genuine student intent

about.label: Application streams
about.value: SDS (where eligible): faster processing with GIC and language scores. Non-SDS: standard study permit pathway.

SDS: GIC + tuition payment proof where required

eligibility.note: SOP and career plan must align

academy_metadata.redFlagsBanner: If refused, do not reapply with the same weak documents. Address the specific refusal reason in writing before a new submission.

redFlags.title: Insufficient or unseasoned funds
redFlags.description: Large recent deposits, funds not aligned with sponsor income, or missing 4–6 month bank history.
redFlags.fix: Season funds early; use GIC for SDS; sponsor ITR + employment letters

redFlags.title: Weak or generic SOP
redFlags.description: Template SOP, course mismatch with past education, or unclear return intent.
redFlags.fix: Course-specific SOP; link prior study/work to Canada program; clear post-study plan

redFlags.title: Wrong stream (SDS vs Non-SDS)

redFlags.fix: Re-check SDS eligibility by country of residence; switch stream and re-quote fees

redFlags.title: LOA / DLI issues
redFlags.description: Expired LOA, non-DLI institution, or start date passed.
redFlags.fix: Verify DLI list on canada.ca; request updated LOA from institution

redFlags.title: Previous refusals not explained
redFlags.description: Canada or other country refusals not disclosed or addressed in cover letter.
redFlags.fix: Full disclosure + changed circumstances + stronger evidence package

redFlags.title: Medical or biometrics overdue
redFlags.description: BIL expired or medical not completed in time.
redFlags.fix: Track BIL deadline; book VAC slot immediately; upload confirmation

faqs.q: Can we switch from SDS to Non-SDS if SDS is paused?
faqs.a: Yes, when the client is SDS-ineligible or SDS is unavailable. Rebuild the file per Non-SDS checklist and update fee quote (no GIC requirement unless otherwise applicable).

faqs.q: How much funds to show for Non-SDS?
faqs.a: Tuition for first year plus living expenses per IRCC guidelines (amounts update—verify on canada.ca). Include credible source of funds and history.
As soon as possible after Biometric Instruction Letter (BIL), typically within 30 days. Counselor should track VAC appointment confirmation.
May require separate applications (visitor permit, OWP, study permit for children). Assess dependants case-by-case; extra funds and ties evidence needed.

faqs.q: What if LOA start date is near?
faqs.a: Apply urgently; explain delay if any. If start date passed, obtain updated LOA before submission.
No—depends on country of residence and stay duration. Follow IRCC medical instructions for the client's profile.

faqs.q: Can client work on study permit?
faqs.a: Generally on-campus without separate permit; off-campus rules have hour limits—cite current IRCC rules at time of counseling.

faqs.q: What happens after study permit approval?
faqs.a: Client receives introduction letter; permit issued at port of entry or as directed. Remind client to maintain full-time status and keep contact details updated with IRCC.

academy_metadata.shortDescription: IRCC temporary resident visa · Tourism, family, business · Biometrics may apply

policyAlert.summary: Confirm current IRCC visitor visa fee (CAD $100) and biometrics fee on canada.ca before quoting clients.

alert.title: Purpose must match documents
alert.body: Tourism, family visit, and business visitor files need different evidence. Misaligned purpose is a common refusal reason—align invitation, itinerary, and cover letter.

about.label: Description
about.value: Temporary Resident Visa (TRV) allowing Indian nationals to visit Canada for tourism, family visits, or short business trips. Most applications are submitted online through the IRCC portal.

about.label: Eligible applicants
about.value: Valid passport · Genuine temporary purpose · Strong ties to India · Sufficient funds · Clean immigration history

about.label: Single vs multiple entry
about.value: Officer decides entry type and validity at approval. Do not promise multiple-entry unless client profile supports it.

about.label: Key authority
about.value: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC)

about.label: After approval
about.value: TRV counterfoil in passport; client must respect stay duration and purpose. Overstay affects future Canada applications.

eligibility.note: Check expiry before booking VAC appointment
Itinerary, invitation letter, or business documents as applicable

eligibility.criterion: Proof of funds for trip duration
eligibility.note: Bank statements, ITR, sponsor letter if applicable
Book within 30 days of BIL if required
Based on residence history and stay plans

eligibility.criterion: No prior misrepresentation or serious inadmissibility

academy_metadata.redFlagsBanner: If refused, do not reapply immediately with the same weak ties evidence. Address the specific refusal reason before a new submission.

redFlags.title: Weak home-country ties
redFlags.description: Unemployed applicant with no property, family, or business ties; vague return plan.
redFlags.fix: Document employment, business, property, family obligations; employer leave letter

redFlags.title: Insufficient or unseasoned funds
redFlags.description: Large recent deposits, funds not matching income, or no sponsor proof.
redFlags.fix: 4–6 month bank history; sponsor ITR + employment letter; explain large credits

redFlags.title: Purpose mismatch
redFlags.description: Tourism file with business documents only, or family visit without host invitation.
redFlags.fix: Align purpose, cover letter, itinerary, and supporting documents

redFlags.title: Previous refusals not addressed
redFlags.description: Canada or other country refusals hidden or not explained in cover letter.
redFlags.fix: Full disclosure + changed circumstances + stronger evidence

redFlags.title: Overstated stay or vague itinerary
redFlags.description: Long stay without credible reason or no hotel/flight bookings where appropriate.
redFlags.fix: Realistic itinerary; explain accommodation; show return ticket intent

redFlags.title: Biometrics deadline missed
redFlags.description: BIL expired before VAC appointment completed.
redFlags.fix: Book biometrics within days of BIL; track confirmation

faqs.q: Can we guarantee approval for a strong profile?
faqs.a: No. IRCC officers assess each case individually. We can improve file quality but never promise approval.

faqs.q: How much funds should a tourist show?
faqs.a: Enough to cover flights, accommodation, and daily expenses for the planned stay. Verify current IRCC guidance; typically CAD $2,000–5,000+ depending on duration and who pays.

faqs.q: Is an invitation letter mandatory for family visits?
faqs.a: Not always legally required, but strongly recommended when visiting relatives. Host should provide invitation, status proof, and address details.
Yes, when circumstances or evidence materially improve. Reapply without addressing refusal reasons usually fails again.

faqs.q: Does TRV allow work in Canada?
faqs.a: No. TRV is for temporary visit only. Work requires a work permit unless exempt.

faqs.q: How long can a visitor stay?
faqs.a: Officer sets duration at port of entry—often up to 6 months per entry. Client must not overstay.
As soon as possible after Biometric Instruction Letter, typically within 30 days.

faqs.q: Can TRV be used to join a spouse studying in Canada?
faqs.a: Sometimes, but assess whether visitor visa or dependant permit is more appropriate. Separate pathways may apply.

academy_metadata.shortDescription: IRCC family sponsorship · Inland & outland · PR pathway for spouses

policyAlert.summary: Confirm current IRCC spousal sponsorship fees (principal + right of permanent residence fee) on canada.ca before quoting.

alert.title: Genuine relationship evidence
alert.body: Officers scrutinise relationship genuineness. Weak or inconsistent evidence is a leading refusal factor—build a chronological, document-backed story.

about.label: Description
about.value: Family class sponsorship allowing a Canadian citizen or permanent resident to sponsor a spouse, common-law, or conjugal partner for permanent residence. Outland applications processed from abroad; inland if partner is in Canada.

about.label: Eligible applicants
about.value: Legally married or qualifying common-law/conjugal relationship · Sponsor meets income/residency requirements · Genuine relationship · Medical and security clearances

about.label: Inland vs outland
about.value: Outland: partner outside Canada, can visit during processing. Inland: partner in Canada; may qualify for open work permit while awaiting decision.

about.label: Key authority
about.value: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC)

about.label: After approval
about.value: Partner receives COPR and completes landing. Maintain relationship genuineness obligations; misrepresentation has serious consequences.

eligibility.criterion: Sponsor is Canadian citizen or PR aged 18+
eligibility.note: Verify sponsor status and residency obligations

eligibility.criterion: Legally valid marriage or qualifying relationship
eligibility.note: Marriage certificate; common-law needs 12+ months cohabitation proof

eligibility.criterion: Sponsor not in default of prior sponsorship undertakings

eligibility.note: Both sponsor and applicant if required

academy_metadata.redFlagsBanner: If refused for relationship genuineness, do not reapply with the same weak evidence. Rebuild a chronological, verifiable relationship narrative first.

redFlags.title: Weak relationship evidence
redFlags.description: Few photos, no communication logs, short relationship timeline, or inconsistent stories.
redFlags.fix: Chronological timeline; chat/call logs; joint finances; visit stamps; affidavits from family

redFlags.title: Marriage of convenience suspicion
redFlags.description: Large age gap without explanation, met only days before marriage, or prior failed sponsorships.
redFlags.fix: Honest narrative; explain how relationship developed; strong third-party support

redFlags.title: Incomplete sponsor eligibility
redFlags.description: Sponsor on social assistance, prior undertaking default, or not meeting residency for PR sponsors.
redFlags.fix: Verify sponsor eligibility checklist on canada.ca before fee collection

redFlags.title: Wrong stream (inland vs outland)
redFlags.description: Applied inland when partner cannot remain legally in Canada, or outland without travel plan clarity.
redFlags.fix: Assess partner's current status; choose stream with legal counsel if complex

redFlags.title: Missing or inconsistent forms
redFlags.description: IMM forms incomplete, dates mismatch across documents, or unsigned declarations.
redFlags.fix: Cross-check all dates; dual review before upload

redFlags.title: Undisclosed prior relationships or refusals
redFlags.description: Hidden prior marriages, refusals, or misrepresentation history.
redFlags.fix: Full disclosure; address changed circumstances in cover letter

faqs.q: Can we guarantee PR approval for a genuine marriage?
faqs.a: No. Even genuine couples can face requests for additional evidence or refusal if documentation is weak. We improve file quality but never promise approval.
Timelines vary and change. Outland may allow partner to visit during processing; inland may offer open work permit eligibility. Assess case-by-case.

faqs.q: How much relationship evidence is enough?
faqs.a: No fixed amount—officers want a credible chronological story: how you met, communication, visits, marriage, shared life plans. Quality over quantity.

faqs.q: Can a sponsored spouse work while waiting?
faqs.a: Inland applicants may apply for open work permit after AIP stage in many cases. Verify current IRCC rules at time of counseling.
Recent marriages need stronger evidence—communication history before marriage, visit records, photos, and explanation of relationship development.
Spousal sponsorship generally has no LICO requirement for spouse/partner/child, but sponsor must not be on social assistance (except disability) and must meet undertaking obligations.
Review refusal letter carefully. Options may include appeal (IAD) for in-Canada refusals in some cases, or reapplication with stronger evidence.
Yes, if they have cohabited continuously for at least 12 months. Evidence of shared residence is essential.

alert.title: One-time PGWP eligibility
alert.body: PGWP can usually only be obtained once. Program length and DLI status determine permit duration—verify before promising 3-year PGWP.

about.label: Description

alert.body: Most employer-specific permits require LMIA from ESDC unless exempt (ICT, CUSMA, PGWP, etc.). Verify category before quoting timeline.
